what_it_does: It is a prescriptive build plan for a GPU/serving/loadout rework, organized into verify-first spikes and seven implementation phases, each with a function, verification criteria, and dependencies.
observations: A VERIFY-FIRST phase (0.1-0.6) gates later work with measured answers.
The FP8 brain is mid-audit, so Phases 1-4 need a clear card or a coordinated window.
The budget becomes a block-aware measured-once table rather than a live token formula.
sleep-mode and KV-offload are treated as de-risked, not banked.
enforce-eager is configured per-loadout rather than globally.
Every phase ends with a verified-by-use checklist using real boots, turns, or measured numbers.
Code-only sub-steps are allowed to land anytime, while card-dependent steps are blocked.
Phase 7 queues ~42 pre-existing red acceptance suites as a separate cleanup track.
standouts: VERIFY-FIRST spikes gate all downstream design decisions.
Measured, block-aware KV budget replaces hand-set gpu_util values.
Loadouts gain explicit shape fields (max_model_len, max_num_seqs, enforce_eager, priority, co-residency).
Card-sharing design is conditional on a real sleep-mode measurement.
conventions: Phased build plan with numbered milestones and checkboxes.
Each phase states a function and a verification clause.
Explicit dependency graph at the end.
Frontmatter with type, register, aliases, tags, status, and coverage.
References external documents and internal terms without defining them.
concerns: status is 'unconfirmed' and many phases depend on unaudited spikes.
Phase 0 packs six distinct verification spikes that could each block large downstream scope.
Phases 1-4 require a 'clear card' but no schedule or owner is given.
Phase 7 is a vague bucket ('~42 pre-existing red acceptance suites') with no detail.
Assumes familiarity with internal names (Tim, RHM, MCP, company CLI, requires-gate spine).
notes: This is a verification-gated, measurement-driven build plan for turning hand-tuned GPU loadouts into profiled, selectable, documented configurations.
questions: What exactly is the 'FP8 brain' audit covering and when will it finish?
What do 'RHM', 'MCP', 'Tim', and 'requires-gate spine' refer to in this repo?
What is the current implementation of 'company profile <key>' and the loadout schema?
What are the 42 red acceptance suites and why are they queued separately?
What is the precise meaning of '/sleep level 1' and the expected freed-VRAM metric?
purpose_vs_actual: matches — it presents itself as a build plan and does so.
apparent_themes: CLI and documentation as first-class deliverables
intention_signals: It is trying to replace hand-set GPU utilization and static concurrency limits with measured profiles and computed KV budgets, make loadouts explicit and selectable across CLI/MCP/RHM, and force every design choice to be proven by real use.
novelty: A block-aware, measured-once budget table derived from torch-allocator APIs and vLLM debug logs; per-loadout enforce-eager and shape fields; explicit verify-first spikes as project gates; honest reload-based switching if sleep-mode fails.
contribution: It contributes a phased, verification-gated work breakdown for moving GPU serving from heuristic configuration to measured, per-loadout budgets and selectable loadouts, including the profiler, budget computation, loadout shape schema, selection surfaces, and documentation requirements.
summary_for_embedding: A phased build plan for reworking GPU serving and model loadouts, centered on verify-first spikes, a measured profiler, block-aware computed KV budgets, loadout shape fields, card-sharing, selection surfaces, and CLI/docs, with explicit verification criteria and dependencies.
intention_for_embedding: To replace hand-guessed GPU configuration with measured, per-loadout, verified serving budgets and first-class loadout selection across CLI, MCP, and RHM.
